title: 10 best herbal decoctions for cough
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for cough are a natural remedy that involves steeping herbs in hot water to create a soothing liquid extract. These decoctions have been used for centuries to alleviate symptoms of cough, such as congestion and inflammation, by providing expectorant properties that help loosen mucus and reduce coughing fits. Examples include elderberry, ginger, and thyme decoctions, which can be consumed warm or sweetened with honey. By using herbal decoctions, people can find relief from chronic coughs, sleep better, and enjoy improved overall health, leading to a significant improvement in their daily lives.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with cough because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe irritated mucous membranes in the throat and lungs. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root has been shown to reduce inflammation and congestion, making it easier to expel phlegm and mucus from the body. Additionally, licorice decoctions have natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear out stubborn coughs and bronchitis, providing relief from constant hacking and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, which can alleviate coughing caused by respiratory infections.
Expectorant properties: The saponins present in licorice root help loosen and expel mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up phlegm and relieving congestion.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Licorice root's bioactive compounds have been shown to exhibit antiviral and antibacterial activities against various pathogens that can cause respiratory infections, which can help combat the underlying infection causing the cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps soothe and calm coughs.
Rhyzomes: Contain triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that relieve cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried licorice root powder 20-30 grams and water 2 cups.
Boil the water in a pot for 10 minutes then reduce heat.
Add the licorice root powder to the boiling water stirring well.
Simmer the mixture for 15-20 minutes or until it reaches desired strength.
Strain the decoction and let it cool before serving hot.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow jessamine decoctions helps with cough because it has natural expectorant properties that help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel. The decoction's antispasmodic effects also help to relax the bronchial muscles, reducing coughing spasms and providing relief from congestion and discomfort. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may help to soothe the mucous membranes, further reducing inflammation and irritation that can exacerbate a persistent cough.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Yellow Jessamine decoctions help to loosen and expel mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, which helps alleviate discomfort and soothe a sore throat associated with coughing.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Yellow Jessamine's antiviral and antibacterial agents help to combat infections that cause coughs, such as respiratory viruses and bacterial infections like bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help soothe and calm irritated respiratory tract tissues, reducing inflammation and relieving cough symptoms.
Barks: Contain tannins and saponins that work synergistically to reduce mucus production, ease congestion, and provide a calming effect on the bronchial passages.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10-15 grams of dried gelsemium sempervirens leaves and stems in a clean glass container.
Combine the dried herb mixture with 500ml of boiling water in a large ceramic pot.
Simmer the mixture for 20-30 minutes or until the liquid has reduced to 250ml.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth into a separate glass container and discard the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool down to room temperature before storing it in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder decoctions helps with cough because of its natural expectorant properties, which loosen and clear out mucus from the airways. The antiviral and anti-inflammatory compounds in elder flowers help to soothe a sore throat and reduce congestion, making it easier to breathe and cough up excess mucus. Additionally, elder's antimicrobial properties can help combat infections that cause coughs, providing relief and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Elder decoctions contain compounds like anthocyanins and flavonoids that have antiviral properties, which help combat viruses that cause coughs.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The polyphenolic compounds present in elder flowers have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways associated with coughing.
Expectorant properties: Elder decoctions contain mucilages and saponins that help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel phlegm and relieve cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm irritated respiratory tissues.
Rhyzomes: Contain mucilage, a natural expectorant that helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sambucus nigra flowers, leaves, and stems in quantities of one tablespoon per 8 ounces water.
Rinse the plant material under cold running water to remove impurities and debris thoroughly.
Combine the rinsed sambucus nigra plant material with boiling water in a heat-resistant container, steeping for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids completely.
Allow the liquid to cool before refrigerating or freezing for later use within three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower decoctions helps with cough because it contains potent compounds like triterpenoids, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm irritated airways. The decoction's antiviral and antimicrobial properties also help to combat the underlying infection or inflammation causing the cough. Additionally, its expectorant action helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, providing relief from congestion and coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Purple coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like phenolic acids, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and ease congestion associated with a cough.
Antiviral activity: The decoction has been shown to exhibit antiviral properties, which can help combat viral infections that cause coughs, such as the common cold and flu.

Soothing mucilage: Purple coneflower contains a type of sugar molecule called mucilage, which forms a protective coating in the throat and airways, soothing irritation and providing relief from coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making them effective in relieving cough symptoms.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the purple coneflower are rich in bioactive compounds that help to soothe irritated mucous membranes and reduce coughing spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers in sufficient quantity for desired dosage.
Combine one teaspoon of dried echinacea flowers or two teaspoons of fresh flowers with one cup boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes then strain and discard solids or squeeze through cheesecloth.
Allow the liquid to cool and then refrigerate it for at least four hours before consumption.
Drink the decoction three times a day as needed for relief from cough and congestion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme decoctions helps with cough because it contains thymol, a potent expectorant that loosens and clears mucus from the lungs. The warm, soothing properties of the decoction also ease congestion and reduce inflammation in the throat and chest, providing fast relief from persistent coughing. Additionally, thyme's antimicrobial properties help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the cough, promoting a faster recovery and alleviating discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Thyme's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial and viral infections that cause cough, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.
Mucolytic effects: Thyme's mucolytic properties break down mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion and coughing fits associated with respiratory infections.
Anti-inflammatory action: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can exacerbate cough symptoms and make breathing more difficult.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, thyme roots have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making them effective in soothing and relieving cough.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain volatile oils and flavonoids that help to calm the throat and bronchial passages, reducing inflammation and alleviating cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried thymus vulgaris leaves for every cup of boiling water.
Combine the measured thyme and boiling water in a clean glass pot and let it steep.
Allow the mixture to simmer on low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid reduces slightly.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding solids.
Let the decoction cool to room temperature before refrigerating or freezing for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint decoctions helps with cough because of its natural soothing properties that ease congestion and inflammation in the chest and throat. The menthol content in peppermint acts as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of coughing fits. Additionally, peppermint's cooling sensation can help numb the throat, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort associated with coughing.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Peppermint's decoction helps loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieve congestion.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The menthol content in peppermint has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it less uncomfortable to cough and reducing pain.

Natural analgesic and anesthetic properties: Peppermint's menthol also provides natural relief from coughing fits by numbing the mucous membranes and throat, providing temporary relief from discomfort and pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of peppermint contain a high concentration of menthol, which helps to ease congestion and calm coughs.
Barks: The barks of peppermint are rich in tannins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritated throats and reduce coughing.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the menthol and flavonoids to infuse.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup or bottle.
Let the decoction cool slightly before consuming it warm or at room temperature.
Drink the peppermint tea two to three times daily as needed to alleviate cough symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel decoctions helps with cough because of its expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways. The herb's volatile oils, such as anethole and fenchone, have natural anti-inflammatory effects that soothe irritated mucous membranes and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. Additionally, fennel decoctions can help to thin and expel phlegm, providing relief from coughing fits and congestion, making it a natural remedy for alleviating symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Fennel decoction helps loosen and clear out mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing fits.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, which can help alleviate coughing caused by irritated tissues.
Antispasmodic properties: Fennel's antispasmodic compounds relax the smooth muscle cells in the respiratory tract, helping to relieve spasms and coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have been traditionally used to soothe a dry, hacking cough and provide relief from respiratory congestion.
Bark: The bark of the fennel plant contains compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, making it effective in treating a persistent cough.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried foeniculum vulgare seeds for every cup of boiling water needed.
Combine the measured seeds with boiling water in a medium-sized pot and steep for 5 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a separate container to remove solids.
Allow the decoction to cool slightly before drinking, taking up to three times a day as needed.
Store any leftover decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours and reheat as necessary.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese snakegourd decoctions helps with cough because its unique blend of active compounds, such as flavonoids, terpenes, and alkaloids, possesses natural expectorant properties. These compounds help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, reducing congestion and relieving cough symptoms. Additionally, snakegourd's anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe irritated respiratory tissues and reduce inflammation in the throat, further alleviating cough discomfort. As a result, Chinese snakegourd decoctions have been traditionally used to treat a range of respiratory issues, including chronic bronchitis, asthma, and upper respiratory tract infections.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: The decoction's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce inflammation in the throat, alleviating coughing fits and discomfort.
Expectorant property: The expectorant property helps loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel phlegm and relieving congestion and coughing.

Antiviral property: The antiviral property of the decoction helps combat viral infections that cause cough, such as respiratory syncytial virus (RSV), by inhibiting their replication and reducing the severity of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract and relieving cough.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain bioactive compounds that help to soothe irritated throat mucosa and reduce coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Wash the dried roots of trichosanthes kirilowii thoroughly with clean water to remove impurities.
Measure out one tablespoon of cleaned and crushed trichosanthes kirilowii roots for every 250ml of water.
Combine the measured roots with boiling water in a saucepan and simmer for 30 minutes to extract active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove solids.
Allow the decoction to cool down before serving, consuming warm to help alleviate cough symptoms within an hour of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap decoctions helps with cough because it contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce congestion in the chest and throat. The decoction also contains volatile oils that soothe the respiratory tract, calming a persistent cough and relieving irritation. Additionally, skullcap has natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen mucus and ease its elimination from the body, providing relief from stubborn coughs and clearing the airways for easier breathing.

Expectorant: The saponins present in skullcap help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and alleviate congestion and coughs associated with colds and bronchitis.

Anti-inflammatory: Skullcap's flavonoids and terpenoids have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to chronic coughing and improve overall respiratory health.

Rhyzomes: Containing essential oils and volatile compounds, skullcap rhyzomes have expectorant properties that can help loosen and clear phlegm from the lungs.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried scutellaria lateriflora root in a small bowl.
Combine the root with two cups of filtered water in a medium saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat for five minutes then reduce heat to low simmer.
Allow the decoction to steep for 15-20 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh strainer into a cup and discard the solids after drinking.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein decoctions helps with cough because of its potent expectorant properties, which loosen and clear out mucus from the respiratory tract. The plant's leaves contain saponins and flavonoids that soothe and calm irritated airways, reducing inflammation and congestion. By thinning and removing excess mucus, mullein decoctions help to ease coughing fits and make breathing easier, providing relief from persistent coughs caused by colds, allergies, and other respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Mullein's decoction helps reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, soothing a cough and alleviating discomfort.
Expectorant properties: The mucilages present in mullein's decoction loosen and expel mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up phlegm and relieving congestion.
Antiviral and antimicrobial properties: Mullein's decoction contains compounds that can help combat viral and bacterial infections responsible for coughs, such as the common cold and bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Mullein roots contain mucilages and saponins that help soothe and calm irritated respiratory tract tissues, reducing inflammation and congestion.
Leaves: The leaves of mullein have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried verbascum thapsus leaves and stems in sufficient quantity for desired amount.
Cut the herb into small pieces and grind them into a fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Measure one teaspoon of powdered verbascum thapsus and combine it with one cup of boiling water in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly and the flavor has intensified.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic decoctions helps with cough because of its natural expectorant properties, which loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel. The sulfur compounds in garlic also have anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce congestion and soothe an irritated throat. Additionally, herbal garlic decoctions contain antioxidants that combat infections, such as colds and flu, which can contribute to persistent coughing. Overall, a warm cup of garlic decoction can provide fast relief from the discomfort of a cough.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Garlic decoction helps to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel, thus relieving cough and congestion.
Antimicrobial property: Garlic's antimicrobial compounds inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause coughs, reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting recovery.

Anti-inflammatory property: Garlic decoction has anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and irritation in the respiratory tract, alleviating cough and sore throat associated with upper respiratory infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sulfur compounds, which help to break down mucus and relieve congestion.
Rhyzomes: Contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm irritated throats.
remedy_recipe: Rinse 10-15 fresh garlic cloves under cold running water before peeling and slicing them thinly.
Combine the sliced garlic with 250ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass or ceramic container.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for 5-7 minutes while stirring occasionally.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before refrigerating it for up to 2 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with cough because of its natural expectorant properties, which loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs and airways. The bitter compounds in dandelion root and leaves stimulate the production of saliva and mucus, making it easier to expel phlegm and other debris that can exacerbate a cough. Additionally, dandelion's anti-inflammatory effects may help reduce congestion and discomfort associated with a persistent cough. As a result, drinking dandelion decoctions can provide relief from dry, hacking coughs and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: Dandelion mucilage coats the throat and airways, soothing irritated tissues and reducing inflammation, making it easier to expectorate mucus and relieving coughing.
Inulin: Inulin, a prebiotic fiber found in dandelion roots, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the respiratory tract, easing congestion and alleviating cough symptoms.

Taraxasterols: Taraxasterols, a type of triterpenoid saponin found in dandelion roots, have antitussive properties that help suppress cough reflexes, providing relief from persistent and irritating coughs.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated respiratory tract tissues.
Flowers: The flowers contain flavonoids and terpenes that can help to reduce inflammation and relax the airways, providing relief from coughing.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried taraxacum officinale root into a clean glass jar.
Add one cup of boiling water to the jar and let it steep for ten minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container.
Discard the solids and let the decoction cool to room temperature within thirty minutes.
Transfer the cooled decoction to an airtight container and store it in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower decoctions helps with cough because of its natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The herbal remedy, containing Echinacea angustifolia, has been traditionally used to soothe irritated throats and lungs, reducing congestion and mucus production. As a result, it provides relief from persistent coughs and helps to loosen phlegm, allowing for easier breathing and improved overall respiratory health. By calming the airways, Kansas coneflower decoctions can also help reduce the severity of symptoms associated with colds and flu.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Kansas Coneflower has antiviral properties that help combat the underlying viral infection causing the cough, reducing its severity and duration.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the airways and throat, easing congestion and discomfort associated with a persistent cough.
Expectorant action: Kansas Coneflower's saponins have a natural expectorant effect, loosening mucus and phlegm in the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up and relieve congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Kansas coneflower plant have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including coughs, due to their antitussive and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: The buds of the Kansas coneflower plant contain salicylic acid, which has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe a sore throat and reduce coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried echinacea angustifolia root in the morning after drying for 3-6 months, storing them in an airtight container.
Measure 2 tablespoons of dried echinacea angustifolia root and place it in a heat-resistant glass bowl or teapot.
Boil 4 cups of pure filtered water in a pot, then slowly pour the boiling water over the herbal mixture, allowing it to steep for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the decoction using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large heat-resistant glass measuring cup, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Pour the cooled decoction into a clean glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, storing it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days to consume as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Selfheal decoctions helps with cough because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antitussive properties. The decoction's active compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, soothe the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and congestion that often accompany a cough. Additionally, selfheal's expectorant properties help loosen and expel phlegm, making it easier to clear the airways and alleviate coughing fits. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Self-heal contains flavonoids, such as hyperoside and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate cough symptoms.
Expectorant properties: The decoction of self-heal has been traditionally used to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel phlegm and reducing cough severity.

Antibacterial properties: Self-heal contains compounds with antibacterial activity, such as rosmarinic acid, which can help combat secondary bacterial infections that may exacerbate coughs and respiratory issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, roots are used to treat respiratory issues, including coughs, due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds like isothiocyanates and phenolic acids, rhyzomes help alleviate cough symptoms by reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried prunella vulgaris leaves and flowers in sufficient quantities.
Combine the harvested material with one quart of boiling water and steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Allow the liquid to cool and then refrigerate it at 40°f or below until consumption.
Administer the cooled decoction as needed up to three times daily in doses of one ounce.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain decoctions helps with cough because it soothes and calms the throat, reducing inflammation and irritation. The plantain's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling in the respiratory tract, making it easier for the body to expel mucus and phlegm. Expectorant properties: The mucilage present in plantain decoctions acts as an expectorant, loosening mucus and phlegm in the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion and coughing.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help soothe and calm irritated throat tissues, reducing coughing fits.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and tannins, the barks have astringent properties that aid in drying up excess mucus and reducing cough severity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried plantago major leaves from a trusted source weighing approximately one ounce.
Rinse the plant material gently with cold filtered water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine the rinsed plantago major with four cups of boiling distilled water in a large glass pot and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids immediately.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature and store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers for every 8 ounces of water.
Combine the measured lavender flowers with the water in a medium saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil and then reduce heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a large glass measuring cup.

remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried mentha aquatica leaves and stems, fresh or dried.
Combine the ingredients with 8 ounces of boiling water in a large glass container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve.
Add one tablespoon of honey and mix well, let it cool to room temperature before serving.
Store any remaining decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried melissa officinalis leaves weighing approximately 2-3 teaspoons per cup.
Combine the dried leaves with boiling water in a teapot or infuser to create a decoction.

This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe and calm irritated respiratory tissues.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Ginger's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel, thereby reducing congestion and relieving cough symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with a cough.

Antibacterial and antiviral properties: Ginger has been shown to have antibacterial and antiviral activity, which helps to combat infections that can cause coughs, such as bronchitis, pneumonia, and the common cold.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizome, which is the underground stem, is commonly used to make decoctions for cough relief due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Roots: Ginger roots are used to make decoctions that help alleviate cough symptoms by reducing inflammation in the throat and chest.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots of approximately 2-3 inches in length for each dose.
Wash the ginger roots thoroughly with cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Peel the ginger roots using a vegetable peeler or sharp knife to expose the inner layer.
Cut the peeled ginger roots into small pieces weighing about 1/4 teaspoon per 8 ounces of boiling water.
Simmer the ginger pieces in boiling water for 10-15 minutes to create a decoction for 3 times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Henbane decoctions helps with cough because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's active compounds, such as scopoletin and hyoscyamine, work together to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by a persistent cough. This natural remedy can also help loosen mucus and phlegm, making it easier to expel, providing relief from chest congestion and hacking coughs.
remedy_properties: Antitussive (cough-suppressing) properties: Henbane's decoction helps suppress coughing by reducing the irritability of the bronchial mucosa, making it less likely for the cough to persist or worsen.

Expectorant properties: Henbane's decoction loosens and removes mucus from the airways, allowing for easier clearance of respiratory congestion and alleviating coughing fits caused by excessive phlegm.

Sedative properties: Henbane's decoction has a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to reduce stress, anxiety, and restlessness often associated with coughs, promoting better sleep and relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making them effective in relieving chest congestion and cough symptoms.
Seeds: Rich in essential oils and alkaloids that help soothe a dry, irritated throat and calm cough reflexes.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 30 grams of dried hyoscyamus niger roots.
Combine the measured root material with 1 liter of boiling water in a pot.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let it steep for 15-20 minutes allowing the mixture.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container.
Store the prepared decoction in an amber-colored glass bottle at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena decoctions helps with cough because of its natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The herbal remedy soothes an irritated throat, reducing inflammation and congestion caused by a cough. As it warms the chest, it loosens mucus, allowing for easier expulsion and relief from congestion. Additionally, lemon verbena's antispasmodic effects calm spasms in the bronchial tubes, providing fast and effective relief from a persistent cough. This natural remedy offers a gentle yet potent solution for soothing a cough and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Lemon verbena's antiviral property helps combat viral respiratory infections, such as the common cold and flu, which are often responsible for coughs.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory effects help reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, relieving discomfort and pain associated with coughing.

Expectorant action: Lemon verbena's expectorant property helps loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel phlegm and relieve congestion, which can contribute to chronic coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon verbena have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and reducing irritation caused by cough.
Seeds: Lemon verbena seeds contain oils that help to loosen and expel mucus from the lungs, providing relief from cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Collect high-quality dried verbena officinalis leaves and flowers, measuring out 2 teaspoons for every cup of water.
Rinse the verbena mixture with cold filtered water, gently swirling it around before draining off any impurities.
Bring 8 ounces of purified water to a gentle boil in a medium-sized saucepan, reducing heat once boiling commences.
Add the rinsed verbena mixture to the simmering water and let steep for 15 minutes, allowing the herbal properties to infuse.
Strain the decoction using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding solids and transferring the liquid to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Joe pye weed decoctions helps with cough because it contains a unique compound called beta-amyrin triterpenoid, which has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and antitussive properties. When consumed as a decoction, this compound can help soothe irritated mucous membranes in the throat and lungs, reducing inflammation and easing coughing fits. Additionally, joe pye weed's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear out mucus, providing quick relief from congestion and chest tightness associated with coughs.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Joe Pye Weed decoctions help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to a range of breathing difficulties, including chronic coughs.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Joe Pye Weed's antiviral and antibacterial components help to combat viral and bacterial infections that can cause persistent coughing, such as bronchitis, pneumonia, and the common cold.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried eupatorium perfoliatum root per cup of boiling water.
Combine the measured herbs and water in a medium-sized saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes or until the decoction reaches desired strength.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh strainer into a large bowl, discarding solids.
Allow the decoction to cool and then transfer it to an amber glass bottle for storage in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow decoctions helps with cough because of its soothing and demulcent properties. The saponins in marshmallow root create a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by coughing. This allows for easier clearance of mucus from the respiratory tract, alleviating congestion and coughing fits. Additionally, marshmallow's natural anti-inflammatory properties help to calm irritated airways, providing relief from hacking coughs and promoting a restful night's sleep.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow decoction contains high amounts of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and lubricates the mucous membranes in the throat and airways, providing relief from coughing and irritation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and saponins present in marshmallow decoction have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the respiratory tract, further alleviating cough symptoms.

Expectorant action: Marshmallow decoction has a mild expectorant property, which means it helps loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and reducing the frequency of coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm irritated throat tissues, making it an effective ingredient in decoctions for cough.
Seeds: Marshmallow seeds have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, with their medicinal properties helping to alleviate cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Rinse one tablespoon of dried marshmallow root with cold water.
Combine the rinsed marshmallow root with two cups of boiling water in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture for 15-20 minutes or until the liquid has reduced by half.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup.
Let the decoction cool to room temperature before consuming it three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bay rum decoctions helps with cough because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried pimenta racemosa leaves in the morning for maximum potency.
Measure out 20-30 grams of the herb and chop it into small pieces to enhance extraction.
Combine the chopped pimenta racemosa with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 15-20 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container and store it in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey decoctions helps with cough because of its expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of symphytum officinale, clean and chop them into small pieces for use.
Combine one teaspoon of chopped herb with two cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes before straining and discarding solids to create the decoction.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature, then store it in an airtight container for up to three days.
Drink one-quarter cup of the cooled decoction two to three times a day as needed.

remedy_recipe: Gently collect and rinse 10-15 grams of fresh rumex crispus leaves in cold running water.

Combine the chopped leaves with 500ml of boiling water in a large ceramic pot.
Allow the mixture to simmer for 10-15 minutes, then reduce heat and steep for an additional 20 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa rugosa flowers and leaves according to your local climate and plant availability guidelines for 30g.
Combine the harvested flora with 600ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container at a ratio of 1:10.
Steep the mixture for 15-20 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the hot water and reduce to 300ml.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids after pressing on the herbs with the back of spoon to extract all liquid.
Store the resulting herbal decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and take 100ml three times daily to alleviate cough symptoms.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat cough with herbal decoctions is by combining potent herbs like ginger, slippery elm, and licorice root. Simmer these ingredients in hot water for 10-15 minutes to create a soothing concoction. Strain the liquid and sweeten it with honey to enhance its effects. Drink the decoction several times a day to relieve cough symptoms and reduce inflammation in the throat.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cough that are treated with herbal decoctions are sore throat, bronchitis, and colds. Herbal decoctions such as thyme, ginger, and licorice root help soothe a sore throat, while those containing eucalyptus, peppermint, and lemon balm relieve congestion in the chest associated with bronchitis and colds. These natural remedies provide relief from discomfort, inflammation, and respiratory issues without harsh chemicals or side effects.
